name: awen
description: A Qt Quick application framework and the apps built on it.

dependencies.$comment: The test binaries are built and run natively; there is no runner on wasm, and an android cross-build cannot execute the binary gtest_discover_tests runs at build time.
dependencies.name: gtest

dependencies.$comment: Host-tool Qt (qmlcachegen, qsb, moc) with the default features (icu, openssl, sql, dbus, widgets, ...) stripped; the ports re-add the few they need (e.g. qtshadertools adds gui). thread + concurrent are kept explicitly: host qtdeclarative only builds its qmlls MODULE when QT_FEATURE_thread is on, but still builds the qmlls TOOL that links it unconditionally, so without thread the host qtdeclarative fails to configure ('Qt6::QmlLSPrivate ... target was not found'). The port forces FEATURE_thread=OFF for any feature it does not list, so thread must be named directly; concurrent (which pulls future) matches what QmlLSPrivate actually uses. The desktop presets mask this because host == target there merges the target features (which pull thread) into this same qtbase; the wasm cross-build is the first host-only qtbase, where nothing else re-adds it.
dependencies.name: qtbase

dependencies.$comment: Target Qt trimmed to what Qt Quick rendering needs on each platform's default backend: OpenGL on desktop, GLES2 in the browser, xcb windowing + fontconfig font discovery on linux, freetype on wasm (no system fonts in the browser sandbox). qtdeclarative itself re-adds network and testlib.
dependencies.name: qtbase

features.$comment: Every platform, wasm included: the port maps this to Qt's umbrella FEATURE_opengl, which is what compiles QPlatformOpenGLContext. gles2 only sets FEATURE_opengles2 and INPUT_opengl='es2' (it selects the backend, it does not enable OpenGL), so dropping opengl here builds a Qt whose own wasm platform plugin fails to compile against the missing QPlatformOpenGLContext.
features.name: opengl

features.$comment: Selects the GLES backend in place of desktop GL: WebGL in the browser, EGL/GLES on android. On android this documents intent rather than changes the graph — the qtbase port's gui feature already pulls egl/freetype/gles2/opengl there; on emscripten it is load-bearing.
features.name: gles2

features.$comment: No fontconfig outside desktop linux: the browser sandbox has no system fonts at all, and the android QPA feeds /system/fonts through QFreeTypeFontDatabase — all android text goes through the FreeType engine.
features.name: freetype

features.$comment: Not needed by Quick, but qtbase 6.11 only links libresolv into QtNetwork under dnslookup AND libresolv (src/network/CMakeLists.txt), while qhostinfo_unix.cpp needs it under libresolv alone - without dnslookup the QtNetwork dylib fails to link (undefined _res_9_ninit) on macOS/Linux. Dependency-free, so keeping it restores Qt's tested configuration. Excluded on emscripten: the port turns it into FEATURE_libresolv=ON, and the browser sandbox has no resolver, so Qt fails configure with 'Feature libresolv: Forcing to ON breaks its condition'.
features.name: dnslookup

features.$comment: qtbase 6.11 gates the windeployqt tool behind this feature; without it qt_generate_deploy_qml_app_script's install step fails with 'No Qt deploy tool available for this target platform'. macdeployqt is not gated, so only windows needs it.
features.name: windeployqt
